name: Elmslie, T. Thomas
clubs: Assistant to Mr. Olson in Geography
description: Mr. Thomas gives us a glimpse of the sterling quality of Oberlin College. In addition to helping Mr. Olson he assists Mr. Marker when Psychology takes a geographical turn. He has won a host of friends during his stay in Kent and all join in wishing him success in his chosen field.

name: Paulman, Louise
major: K. S. N. C. diploma course
description: Having attended so many schools, Miss Paulman is able to look at education with a clear and concise view. She is especially interested in converting Mr. Layton to the modern theory of education.

name: Heriff, Amy Irene
description: Miss Herriff is a teacher of broad experience. By her splendid work in the Kent public schools she has won many friends who are proud to call her teacher.

name: Rohrbaugh, Lillie M.
description: Miss Rohrbaugh is a graduate of Heidelberg College, finishing her course in that institution in 1889. She received the Normal diploma from K. S. N. C. in 1915. She has a fund of experiences gathered from years spent in the public schools of Ohio.

name: Kane, Harry W.
high school: Fredonia Institute Preparatory Department, Kent Normal
description: Mr. Kane comes to us well prepared by his fourteen years of experience in the common schools of Ohio and his wide range of reading. He likes a good joke and you will find him an excellent conversationalist.

name: Miley, Carlton L.
high school: Delaware High School, Kent State Normal
description: Mr. Miley is one of our students who has assumed the responsibility of supporting a family. Although he looks at life from a serious standpoint, he is a good companion and makes you feel that life is worth living. Lest you forget, we remind you that he was a star at right guard on the basketball team.

name: Graves, Floyd V.
description: Floyd is noted for the ever prevailing smile on his face. He teaches part of the time and spends the remainder in K. S. N. C. and in the Grant Six.

name: Jones, F. Ethel
description: Miss Jones is another Normal graduate who is not satisfied with the diploma alone, but is working toward the degree. She graduated from the diploma course in 1916.

name: Tidd, James W.
high school: Williamsfield High School, Kent State Normal College
motto: "By his deeds you will know him."
description: A good student, friend and classmate, we are glad "Jimmie" chose Kent Normal as his place of evolution from an educational standpoint. In order to broaden his views he employs his spare moments in working on the railroad. At right forward Tidd was a bright and shining star.

name: Robison, Henry J.
description: Henry has served his school in many ways and served it well. As editor of the Chestnut Burr he proved his ability as a literary artist. Everything of merit will find in him a loyal booster.

name: Hewitt, Hazel Clare
description: Of all the shining lights, One of the most bright Is our Hazel Hewitt, Who in all things literary Can surely do it.

name: Roberts, Gilbert
description: Mr. Roberts is one of Normal's best known young men. His active work in school functions and excellent scholarship is a credit both to himself and the school. If you want things done, just ask Gilbert to lend a helping hand. As editor-in-chief of the "Annual" he has worked early and late and the "Annual" of 1917 represents his ability to do things and to do them well.

name: Crosky, Anna Elizabeth
motto: "A little nonsense now and then is relished by the wisest men."
description: Graduate of Scio College in Commercial Course. Five years' teaching experience.

name: Lightell, Mary
description: Hey! Who made that noise over there, did you, "Marguerite"? Well you just better cut that out or I'll get Aunt Emily after you.

name: Heim, Mabel D.
motto: "Campuses" are to our Mabel Just a passing anecdote, But her heart is large and happy, And her life one great big joke.
clubs: Vice President of the Athletic Association
description: Big brown eyes that bring the Skinners, And are not afraid of flame, For she stands outside the lobby, Talking of the "Davey" Game.

name: Gonter, Ethel Nydia
description: Ethel is one of our quiet girls in Lowry Hall. She studies hard and knows that perseverance is bound to win. When it comes to "spreads" you want to call on Ethel.

name: Ford, Beth
description: She is ever willing to help with her bright smile. We all know her as one who does her work and does it well.

name: Friedman, Celia
motto: "I have no spur to prick the sides of my intent, but only vaulting ambition, which leaps o'er itself, and falls on the other."

name: Fowler, Birdie Isabel
description: Altho she is a Birdie, how dignified is she; but having many a lofty aim, her thoughts soar upward, free.

name: Sparks, Grace Alpharetta
description: Graduate of Miami University Summer School, 1915. K. S. N. C. Long Summer Term, 1916. Ohio State University, Continuation Summer School, 1916. Two years' teaching experience. Grace is one of our Lowry Hall-ites who is ever ready for a good time.

name: Ilch, Margaret Elizabeth
description: Just our "Peg." Her mind is here in Kent, we know, but her heart is far away.

name: Rowlee, Margaret
description: A maid quite winsome and commanding, with yards and yards of understanding.

name: Ward, Florence Elizabeth
description: Life is quite interesting, Just full of everything. Friendship with girls and boys, Why, life's just full of joys. So take things as they come, From morn till set of sun.

name: Allen, Ruth Ethel
high school: Graduate of Vernon High School. Special Music. Post-graduate of Kinsman High School.
description: Basketball games are the delight of her life and music the goal for which she is striving.

name: Patterson, Hazel Louise
description: She's the jolliest kind of a friend, When you're in for something new. She'll rattle almost anything When others find nothing to do.

name: Rillie, Miriam Beck
description: Studies hard, no time for folly, Earnest worker, always jolly.

name: Ada, C. Zimmerman
high school: Windham High School, 1913. Newton Falls High School
motto: "Under all speech that is good for anything there lies a silence that is better. Silence is as deep as Eternity; Speech is shallow as Time."
description: One year teaching experience.

name: May, Erwin Merrill
description: She laughs, and the world laughs with her, but she does have occasional flashes of silence in which her laugh is not contagious.

name: Harrison, Vera Louise
motto: "Laugh at your friends, and if your friends are sore, So much the better, you may laugh the more."
description: Three summers at K. S. N. C.

name: Heston, Lydia Erma
motto: "A friendly busy sort of lass, Standing high in every class; Friend of all who know her well; A happy future, who can tell?"
clubs: Chairman of Social Service Committee of Y. W. C. A.

name: Parker, Kathryn A.
motto: "A ready tongue, a ready wit, Oh, who has not felt the touch of it?"
description: A popular young lady of Lowry Hall. "Feeds," Ahem.

name: Faust, Veda E.
description: A popular young lady and accomplished art student. Who could want more?

name: Stewart, Bessie Viola
description: Sweetness long drawn out. Bessie is one girl who is faithful in all her duties to K. S. N. C. She always has a good word and a smile for every one.

name: Herriott, Mildred Louise
description: Full of spirit, calm and cool, Mildred came to Normal School. She is a faithful friend in need, Ready to help when it comes to a feed.

name: Jackman, Mary Bernice
description: "Jack" lives at the Inn, (Which is quite a popular Hall), Whose chief delight is to dance at night With "____" at a "Beautie's" Ball.

name: Hostettler, Hazel Marie
clubs: Chairman of Social Committee of the Home Economics Club
description: The maid with the hazel brown eyes. And, Oh! those feeds we have at noons when she says, "Fill up, girls."

name: Rowland, Florence
description: Loyal to K. S. N. C. is she; A worker beyond compare, No matter what the task may be, You always know she's there.

name: Thompson, Helen Cole
description: Another one of our celebrated songsters. There is no doubt that Helen sings all songs well, but "My Little Gray Home in the West" always has first place on this musician's program.

name: Barry, Alice
high school: Madisonville High School, 1914. K. S. N. C. Summer School, 1916.
description: 'Tis Alice, Miss Barry, She's French, quite airy, She hums popular songs The whole day long.

name: Barnett, Grace Florence
clubs: Lowry Hall Department of The Chestnut Burr
description: Here's to the one who is straight and true, Who never can find too little to do. She is busy here and is busy there, Although she seldom has a care.

name: Huston, Nora Opal
motto: "It is useless to waste one’s thoughts in idle words. It is ruthless. So, leave it to the common herds."

name: Korb, Otto J.
description: Our married man. He is the bright and shining example set for us in Education Classes.

name: Fowler, Marion C.
description: Brilliant, studious, sincere maid, White slips are her only grade. Always willing to beguile Weary classmates with a smile.

name: Russell, Marie Ellen
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Athletic Association
description: Pleasant, quiet, friendly, slow to speak, but remember, when she does speak it's always the right thing at the right time.

name: Perry, Kathryn L.
description: Did you ever hear her fret, If her work she could not get? She just settles down and labors So her work will gain all favors.

name: Davis, Esther Ruth
high school: Graduate of Akron Central High School, 1916
motto: "Never to blend our pleasure or our pride With sorrow of the meanest thing that feels."

name: Shaw, Lucile M.
high school: Bath High School, 1914. Teacher in the Bath schools.
motto: "Good humor is the wealth of the soul."
description: A very busy little lady is she.

name: Hanson, Jennie
high school: Graduate of Central High School, Akron, 1916.
motto: "Altho’ her ankle she did sprain, She never grumbled with the pain. And she studied all the day, For hard work to her is play."

name: Cranz, Gracia E.
motto: "One who’ll lend as quickly as she’ll borrow; One who is the same today as tomorrow; One who will share your joy or your sorrow."

name: Shook, Hazel
description: Her heart is quick in kindly acts, She is a faithful friend; Her grace of mind and grace of form, In graceful measure blend, And those who come to know her, Are sure to know her well. May song of joy be sung to you, Where'er you go, Hazel.

name: Nichols, Helen L.
motto: "Her room is common property, Her heart has space for all. Her spreads are joy and misery For all in Lowry Hall."
clubs: Vice President of Garfield Literary Society

name: Dickenson, Edith M.
high school: Zanesville High School, 1910. Graduate, Columbus Kindergarten Training School, 1913. Dresden County Normal School.
description: She studies so much "Miller," It's a wonder it doesn't kill her.

name: Hartman, Grace L.
description: Grace is quite a musical maid, Whose latest name is "Kid." To fame her future path was laid, But fall in love—she did!

name: Gohlke, Selma
motto: "I pity bashful men."
description: She came to us from Baldwin Wallace, where men are not bashful.

name: Wright, Helen V.
motto: "A guardian angel o'er her life presiding, Doubling her pleasure, and her cares dividing."

name: Gamertsfelder, Ina May
high school: Graduate, Tiverton High School, 1912. Danville-Buckeye City High School, 1915.
motto: "It isn't our size that counts, 'tis true, What we are, depends on what we do."
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Athletic Association

name: Summers, Sylvester
clubs: President of Garfield Literary Society, Winter Term
description: Known throughout our college as "Syl." He has always been an earnest worker for our class welfare and has worked long and faithfully as business manager of the "Annual." Although he has a mania for checkerboard shirts, Persian ties and "pretty girls," we feel certain that in the near future he will become a successful business man.

name: Bissell, Ruth Isabell
high school: Two years at Kinsman High School.
motto: "A dash of lively music, A partner and a chance: To be on pleasure bent, And I shall gaily dance."
clubs: Assistant Senior Editor of The Chestnut Burr

name: Crellin, Kathryn Lora
motto: "Mind is the greater lever of all things; thought is the process by which human ends are alternately answered."

name: Nye, Jessie
description: Stately and tall, with large brown eyes which radiate intelligence and good humor.

name: Jacobs, Frank H.
motto: "He knew whatever's to be known, but much more than he knew would own."

name: Mizer, Helen Lucile
motto: "There's never a rose in all the world But makes some green spray sweeter, There's never a wind in all the sky, But makes some bird wing fleeter."
clubs: Executive Board of Household Arts Club
description: Graduate of West Lafayette High School. Four years teaching experience.

name: Rees, Edith E.
motto: "A quiet little mouse whose motto is, 'To study is to learn.'"
description: Graduate of Bedford High School.

name: Bullock, Frances Lyons
motto: "You wonder where was space in college To store away so much of knowledge."
description: Graduate of Cadiz High School. Cadiz County Normal. Summer Terms K. S. N. C., 1914-15-16. Teacher in Cadiz schools.

name: Agnes, Huston
description: A very quiet little maiden who has made herself popular by her sweet and winning disposition.

name: Herbert, Swigart
clubs: Athletic Editor of The Chestnut Burr
description: Herbert has been a valuable addition to the small contingent of fellows at Normal. His college work has always been of sterling quality and his athletic prowess is recognized by all. Here's to a jolly good fellow.

name: Marjorie, Kline
motto: "Thank Heaven, I have a heart that quails not at the thought of meeting men."
description: Three years' teaching experience.

name: Ellen, G. Saunders
motto: "Her modest looks the cottage might adorn, Sweet as the primrose peeps beneath the thorn."
description: Graduate of Somerset High School, 1912. Wooster Summer School, 1914-15-16. Three years' teaching experience.

name: Leanora, F. Ford
motto: "How pleasant it is at the close of day, No follies to have to repent."

name: Lena, Bernard
motto: Clever and quiet, with little to say, Works quite hard the live long day. Always a smile in her own sweet way, This is the way she passes the day.

name: Stuart, Anna
description: A girl of very studious habits and good natured —so they say!

name: Riedinger, Mary
motto: "Nobody ought to have been able to resist her coaxing; and nobody had any business to try. Yet she never seemed to know it was her manner at all. That was the best of it."

name: Grove, Hazel Faye
motto: "Her very frowns are fairer far, Than smiles of other maidens are."

name: Richardson, Hazel E.
high school: Shreve High School; Niles High School
motto: "She touches but the ivory keys And lo! the ivory keys have souls, A wealth of hidden harmonies From every string of metal rolls."; "Happy-go-lucky, fair and free, Nothing there is that bothers me."
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Athletic Association
description: Graduate of Shreve High School. Oberlin Summer School. Teaching experience.; Although she has only been here one year, Hazel has made her presence felt in all circles.
image: Kent State University_1917_25.png; Kent State University_1917_36.png

name: Esterly, Estella Grace
motto: "Them quiet ones sometimes fool ye."
clubs: Treasurer, Y. W. C. A. Home Economics Club
description: Underneath her thoughtful mien is a wealth of wit and jollity.

name: Holcomb, Harriet Lucille
motto: "All things practical for some future time, A regulated household will be a place sublime."
description: Graduate of South High School, Akron, O. Three semesters, University of Akron.

name: Wilbur, Mary E.
motto: "She put aside self-harming heaviness and entertained a cheerful disposition."
description: Graduate of Granger High School. Two years' teaching experience.

name: Maple, Ruby Ellen
motto: "All things I thought I knew; but now confess The more I know I know the less."
description: Graduate of Danville-Buckeye City High School.

name: Frederick, Florence Leola
motto: "She hath a quiet charm, A happy, friendly face; A smile that one is glad To see in any place."
description: Graduate of Buchtel Academy, 1915.

name: Rider, Bess Dunstan
description: With head erect and queenly tread, Not haughty but serene: With smiling face and ready wit, Our Bess has reigned supreme. Climatology she has conquered With its dews, its frost and snow, Miller's Psyche and Nature Study With its Elm Trees, don't you know.

name: Filson, Elsie
motto: "A winning way, a friendly smile, In all, a girl who's quite worth while."
clubs: Cabinet Member, Y. W. C. A.
description: Graduate of Lisbon High School, 1909. Six weeks Ypsilanti Normal School. K. S. N. C. Summer School, 1914-15-16. Seven years' teaching experience.

name: Shipman, Doris
description: A tall and slender lass is she, Full of fun and jollity, Ever ready to beguile Friend or foe with her dimpled smile.

name: Schultz, Minnie
description: Minnie is a bright, sunshiny little lady. We understand that school teaching will not be her lifelong profession, and we wish her success in her new undertaking.

name: Hess, K. Nile
description: Like the Arab, he silently folds his tent and as silently steals away. Hess has the makings of a good business man as well as a psychologist. Hot air, that's the stuff.

name: Finch, Dorotha May
description: A merry, laughing, dancing "Dot," Who with her eyes flirts quite a lot.

name: Andrews, Mary Rachel
description: Mary works with all her might, Managing her teaching right; Yet she never seems to worry, Never seems to fret or hurry.

name: Schlegel, Faith
description: Small of stature though she be, She is always "Faith" to me.

name: Hanks, Dorothy Clark
description: A special Art Student. One whose great talent will win for her a higher place in the near future.

name: Mcmannis, Helen
description: 'Tis "What?" or "Who?" we hear her say, At least one hundred times a day. For know she will and she must know, Her lessons show that this is so. And with it all—she is so dear, We're mighty glad that she is here.

name: Orin, Esther L.
description: Little, but Oh my! This deficiency is well accounted for by a disposition to lend a helping hand. Especially to a veterinarian, who is from Missouri. So you've got to show me.

name: Rowland, Mabel
description: She is one in the K. S. N. C., Who helps us as the need might be; She studies hard from morn 'til night, And wasteth much of the midnight light.

name: Conant, Grace Catherine
clubs: Chairman of News Committee of Y. W. C. A.
Editor of Y. W. C. A. Department of The Chestnut Burr
description: Grace is the girl of highest ambition, Full of wit and intuition; Flowers and baskets in artistic display, Play important parts in her role of the day.

name: Julian, Sara Lawrence
motto: I live to learn, by learning, learn to live; I search in books and take the best they give.

name: Bethel, Elnetta
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Athletic Association
description: Some say that Elnetta has no heart, but I deny it; She has a heart, and gets her lessons by it.

name: Mccabe, Grace Leonore
motto: "Silence is better than empty chatter."
description: Grace has little to say—but when she says it—oh my! She is a general favorite among her classmates and her success as a teacher is assured.

name: Julien, A. J.
description: She's one of us. We're jolly proud to have one so worthy among the crowd.

name: Alter, Maud
description: Maud is faithful to her work, studies hard and does not shirk; soon we'll see her teaching school, governing with the Golden Rule.

name: Bietz, Marie Elizabeth
description: Marie never worries, It doesn't pay. She faces her tasks in a good-natured way.

name: Grafton, Elizabeth M.
motto: "For she is just the quiet kind, whose nature never varies."

name: Johnson, Alfhild Marie
motto: "Happy am I; from care I'm free! Why aren't they all contented like me?"

name: Underwood, Hilda A.
description: Just a little mite of goodness and a happy, smiling face. Lowry Hall, without our Hilda, would not be the same old place; For she helps at every turning, and her smiles and pleasant ways Are the sort we all remember long, long after college days.

name: Kear, Omer G.
motto: "In our school work let's be cheerful, Greet each other with a smile! Let's make life in Kent State Normal Life that's really worth the while!"

name: Quass, Helen E.
motto: "Let us love so well, Our work shall still be sweeter for our love, And still our love be sweeter for our work."
description: Helen always has a smile for everyone.

name: Swartz, Esther Alice
motto: "Who does the best her circumstance allows, Does well, acts nobly; angels could do no more."

name: Totten, Inez
motto: "Tottie" is lovable, clever and gay, She knows just how to get her way. She smiles right often on many a chap, But not for one does she give a snap.

name: Brown, Corinne
motto: "Attempt the end and never stand to doubt; Nothing's so hard but search will find it out."

name: Houser, Ruth Irene
motto: "And along came 'Ruth.'"
description: A steady, ambitious physical training girl who is fair of face, charming in manner and one who will become a success in any work she will undertake. We all know her by her unrestrained gentle manner in the class room and elsewhere.

name: Bell, Grace
motto: "She is a sensible girl with a heart that is true, well liked by her classmates and other folks too."

name: Dean, Marguerite L.
motto: "If she will, she will, and you may depend on it."

name: Iacobucci, Humbert G.
motto: "It is the height of our ambition to become an artist such as he."

name: Misamore, Ina Mearle
motto: "Let's all subtract unpleasant things, Like lesson plans and pains. 'And then,' said she, 'you'll gladly see, That pleasant things remain.'"

name: Baker, A. Lorena
description: Lorena is our cheerful girl of Lowry Hall, with the contralto voice and the auburn hair. Did any of you ever see her excited or worried? No. It is as impossible to separate calm and confidence from Lorena as it is to separate her wit and red hair.

name: Osbun, Ethel M.
motto: "Study is a dreary thing; I would I knew the remedy."
description: Ethel is tall, stately and dignified. One of the "niftiest" girls at K. S. N. C. She likes to study, especially when there's nothing else to do.

name: Laviers, Sarah
description: When we picture Sarah, we think of her as wearing a "middy and bloomers," either blowing a whistle or giving some straightforward command which proves to be quite an instigator of "pep." Trustworthiness and independence seem to be her ideals and she certainly lives up to them to the best of her ability.

name: Van Wagenen, Lura
motto: "No matter what the subject be, For information come to me."
description: Our "Assistant Matron." Ahem!

name: Hindman, Pauline
motto: "Of kindly thoughts and sunny ways, This lady has a store; Her voice is low and pleasant, and you want to hear it more. Her laughing eyes are blue and bright, Her smile is ever sweet; She's dainty as a flower, and affable and neat."

name: Meikle, Mazie M.
motto: "It is not strength, but art, obtains the prize, And to be swift is less than to be wise."

name: Fuller, Martha Vernice
high school: Madison Township High School, Madison Village High School
motto: "Happy art thou, as if every day thou hadst picked up a horseshoe."

name: Knight, Mae
description: A whole-souled, winsome lassie! Her heart has room for all, And you'll be sure to share it, If you're within her call.

name: Bowers, Stella M.
motto: "Begone, dull care, I'm busy."

name: Capes, Verna
description: A friend, tried and true. This little lassie thinks autos, airplanes and submarines are twentieth century necessities, and says she'd enjoy a trip in any of them. But we know there is some one to "Wheeler."

name: Crocker, Clarice C.
motto: "Not much talk—a great, sweet silence."
description: Three years' teaching experience.

name: Walsh, Isabelle M.
high school: Mount Marie High School, 1915, Canton, O.
motto: "To hide the fault I see: That mercy I to others show, That mercy show to me."

name: Sullivan, Anne Elizabeth
high school: Graduate, two year Commercial Course, St. Benedict's Academy, Erie, Pa. Graduate of High School Course, St. Joseph's Academy of West Park, O., 1915.
motto: "With strength and patience all her grievous loads are borne, And from the world's rose-bed she only asks a thorn."

name: Hostettler, Clyde O.
high school: Graduate Winesburg High School, 1911. Wooster Summer School, 1912-13-14.
motto: "The hand that follows intellect can achieve."
clubs: Assistant Principal, Birmingham, O., High School, 1914-15
Pittenger Literary Society, President during winter term

name: Hill, Queen Esther
high school: Graduate of Lisbon High School, 1913. Hiram College, 1913-15.
motto: Dainty, lovable and petite, Everywhere her friends you'll meet. Happy as the lark from morn till night, "Spreads" and shows are her delight.

name: Millard, Beatrice A.
description: Little but mighty, when it comes to feeds and mischief. "Where's all that noise coming from?" "Why, the Niles suite, of course."

name: Thurston, Ruth
description: Talking and laughing all the day thru, Little Ruth Thurston never gets blue. She studies quite hard from morn 'til night, Quizzing "Profs" who call her "Thorndike."

name: Jacob, Helen E.
motto: "Bright as a star when only one is shining in the sky, is the beaming radiance in her hazel eyes."
clubs: Chairman of the Missionary Committee of the Y. W. C. A.

name: Hoehn, Christine H.
motto: "Hands off! I wear a diamond."
description: Christine is one of our hard-working, studious girls. She was never known to come to school without her lessons and is always in the lead.

name: Hopper, William Griffin
description: To know Mr. Hopper is to know a real friend. He is a good conversationalist, musician, student, and a loyal booster of K. S. N. C. Intensely interested in agriculture, he will be of much aid to the young farmers with whom he will come into contact.

name: Galbreath, Ruth Ann
description: She's the completest of all girls, and the neatest and brightest and sweetest. To know Ruth is to love her.

name: Molvine, Klein
motto: "A kind of semi-Solomon, half knowing everything, from the cedar to the hyssop."
description: One year at College of Women, Western Reserve University.

name: Young, Laverne L.
motto: "You know I say just what I think, And nothing more or less. I cannot say one thing and mean another, I will not make believe."
description: Mount Union College, 1913-14.

name: Lower, Agnes R.
motto: "None but herself could be her parallel."
description: With a heart full of kindness and a head full of brains, Agnes has made herself a joy to the Faculty and an indispensable friend to the students. Teacher in Alliance City Schools.

name: Ruth, Mcwaters
description: There lived a maid in Lowry Hall, And she was wondrous wise. She could make an apple tart As big as half her size.

name: Margaret, Turner
description: Small in stature, but large in heart, In parties and dances "Peg" does her part. She has all her lessons, she gets them at school, For taking them home is against "Peg's" rule.

name: Ardis, Pfile
description: She is one person who takes great pleasure in all her tasks. We predict a successful future for her.

name: Mary, Elizabeth Gnau
description: Happy-go-lucky, fair and free. Nothing there is that bothers me.

name: Marguerite, Dickerman
motto: "Few bad habits, faults, or vices, Lives to love and that suffices."
description: We fear Marguerite will never be a schoolma'am.

name: M., Joy Hobart
motto: "Never do today what can be put off 'til the morrow."

name: Olive, Ball
description: Blue eyes of a child. "Frankness" Is Olive and yet so wild, Over all dances and parties galore. Lessons must wait—they have done so before.

name: Edna, Blanche Carrier
description: Here we have a little miss who is quite as congenial and cheerful as one could ever expect to find. We know her to be talented along many lines and class her as being "socially" efficient in all respects.

name: Newlands, Mary W.
description: And so when the girls would tempt me on to sin, I try to squash the tempter's voice that urges me within.

name: Keller, Karl N.
description: When on a field trip he does go And when he stands on a rock to pose, And the water swirls and swirls, Wasn't it a sin, that Keller tumbled in?

name: Covell, Isabelle
motto: "Gaze not upon her for she will giggle!"
description: By the way, she is quite a school teacher.

name: Puffer, Dora
description: A person of few words, but does not think the less. Beneath a sober mien is a touch of roguishness.

name: Doolittle, Ruby
description: A young lady of stately and dignified demeanor, who is strictly down to business. We find that she observes the world from a serious viewpoint, and those who come under her direction will surely get all the "worth whileness" out of life.

name: Williams, Florence
description: Pretty warbler, wake the grove to notes of joy, to songs of love.

name: Paulman, Helen E.
description: An earnest student will make a good teacher. She has a good chance to compare two of Ohio's leading Normals, having been a student in both.

name: Woodworth, Nana
motto: "A laugh is just like sunshine For cheering folks along, The soul grows glad that bears it, Grows better and feels strong."

name: Berman, Belle
high school: High School of Commerce, Cleveland, O., Post Graduate Course at Central High School, Cleveland, O.
motto: "Who is it works so hard at night, Always known to think just right? Belle Berman!"

name: Paulman, Matilda E.
description: Hard work and intense application to duty will make good every time. Matilda is a devotee of both and we know she will succeed.

name: Taber, Mabel
description: Although she has not been with us during our Senior year, she is one of our number who is making good in her teaching. We are glad to be able to count her as one of our Class of '17.

name: Gregory, Lucy
motto: "My tongue within my lips I rein; For who talks much must talk in vain."

name: Larkworthy, Mary F.
description: Full of spirit is our Mary, Never known to be contrary; Wears a diamond on her finger, And we fear she will not linger.

name: Clapsaddle, Rachel
description: She laughs from morning until night, Telling jokes is her delight; But she seriously thinks, I have enough of Science and Art; To Household Research I depart.

name: Wood, Genieva
description: She never trusts to luck, Nor knows the art of bluffing, She either has her lessons Or makes attempts at nothing.

name: Dunn, Willa
description: She is small but mighty. Willa has worked hard on the Kentonian and we all appreciate her efforts. Did you ever see her when she was not happy?

name: Reifsnider, Zoe
motto: "In manner gentle, in affection mild."
description: Six years' teaching experience.
